config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa kontrol, hak minoritas dan orang lemah mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang menghormati kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dari sudut pandang saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dijaga, bukan dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in China I have seen how easily "the greater good" can be used to pressure ordinary people and silence weaker voices. A society does need common rules, stability, and sometimes sacrifice, but if individual rights have no real protection, then the collective can become an excuse for abuse. The strongest society is not one that simply overrides people, but one that balances public interests with clear limits on power and real respect for personal dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Bagi saya, pengalaman di Indonesia justru menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyeragaman, menekan suara minoritas, atau mengabaikan warga kecil yang paling rentan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama dalam situasi darurat atau untuk ketertiban publik, tetapi itu tidak boleh menjadi alasan untuk mengorbankan hak dasar tanpa batas yang jelas. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan umum sambil tetap menghormati martabat dan kebebasan setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in my experience “society first” can too easily become a slogan that excuses pressure on ordinary people. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective responsibility, but I also know how often individual complaints are dismissed as inconvenient when the collective is invoked. Public interests matter, especially in emergencies, yet they should not erase basic rights, because once that line is crossed, weaker people are the first to suffer. A healthy society protects the common good by setting limits on power, not by treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau mengorbankan warga kecil yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang ada keadaan darurat atau urusan ketertiban publik yang menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir ketika kepentingan umum dijaga tanpa menjadikan individu sebagai korban yang mudah dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society’s interests generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, collective goals and social stability do matter, but I have also seen how easily “for the greater good” can become a way to dismiss ordinary people’s concerns and silence weaker voices. Public interests can justify limited, specific restrictions in emergencies, but they should never become a blank check to override basic rights or treat people as disposable. A strong society is one that protects the common good while still drawing hard limits around power and respecting individual dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
